title: lauro-cerasus brittoniana
intro: Lauro-cerasus brittoniana, also known as the mountain laurel, is a flowering evergreen shrub native to eastern North America. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of alkaloids, particularly lauroline, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of mountain laurel include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. Mountain laurel can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote respiratory health and overall well-being.
